subject: ENGLISH
title: Identify Grade 3 level-appropriate sight words
objective: Identify Grade 3 level-appropriate sight words and read them accurately.
introduction: Welcome, detective! 🕵️ Sight words are words we see ALL the time when we read. Words like 'because', 'friend', and 'people' show up again and again. When you know them by sight — without having to sound them out — you become a faster, more confident reader. Today you'll spot sight words, sort them, and read them out loud. Ready to solve the case?

vocabulary.definition: A common word you recognize instantly without sounding out.

activities.instruction: Watch and listen! These words appear in almost every story you read.

feedback.correct: Now you know your target words. Let's practice!

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text: Sight words are common words you recognize instantly. Today's words: because, friend, should, people, around, little, always, another.
activities.narration: Milo says: Let's learn about this! Pay close attention.

activities.instruction: Tap each word card to hear it read aloud, then repeat it!

examples: because — 'I am happy BECAUSE I have a good friend.'
friend — 'My FRIEND and I play together.'
should — 'You SHOULD always try your best.'
people — 'Many PEOPLE live in our barangay.'
around — 'The dog ran AROUND the yard.'
little — 'The LITTLE kitten is so cute!'
always — 'I ALWAYS say po and opo.'
another — 'Can I have ANOTHER rice cake?'

feedback.correct: You found every word! Great detective work.
feedback.retry: There are more words to discover. Keep tapping!

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All eight sight words are listed with example sentences.
activities.prompt: Where is it? Tap to find!
activities.narration: Can you spot it? Tap the correct spot!

activities.instruction: Sort each word: Is it a sight word or not a sight word?

feedback.retry: Some words need to move. Try again!

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Sort each word into sight-word or not-sight-word groups.
activities.prompt: Drag each item to its group.
activities.narration: Let's organize! Which items go where?

activities.instruction: Which word is a sight word that means 'a person you like'?

feedback.correct: Yes! 'Friend' is a common sight word.
feedback.retry: Think about which word you see often in stories.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Choose the sight word from the list.
activities.narration: Milo wants to know: what's the right answer? Pick one!

activities.instruction: Match each sight word to a sentence that uses it!

feedback.correct: Perfect matches! You know these words.
feedback.retry: Try matching the words to the right sentences.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Match each sight word to its sentence.
activities.narration: Match the pairs! Which ones go together?

activities.instruction: Put these steps in order: How do we learn a new sight word?

steps: Use the word in a sentence
Read the word in a story

feedback.correct: That's the right order! See, say, use, read.
feedback.retry: Think about what you do first when you learn a new word.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Order the steps: See, Say, Use, Read.
activities.prompt: What comes first? What comes next?
activities.narration: What's the correct order? Drag to arrange!

items.question: What is this lesson about?
items.narration: Milo asks: What is this lesson about?

items.question: Which word did we learn today?
items.narration: Milo asks: Which word did we learn today?

items.question: What should you do after reading?
items.narration: Milo asks: What should you do after reading?

items.question: Why do we learn new words?
items.narration: Milo asks: Why do we learn new words?

items.question: How can you practice at home?
items.narration: Milo asks: How can you practice at home?

assessment.narration: Milo says: Great job! Now let's see what you remember!

storyIntro: Milo found a book full of new words! "Let's read them together!" he says excitedly.
